item.title: Mobile Writing Workflow Demo
item.description: A multilingual bundle demo for translation switching, cover images, and long-form layout.
item.content:encoded:
This English page is the default version of a multilingual bundle.

What to inspect

  • homepage cards and metadata
  • cover image rendering
  • translation switch visibility
  • reading rhythm on narrow screens

Image test

If both language versions render correctly, the multilingual bundle path is working.

item.title: Cards, Not Walls
item.description: A bilingual bundle demo about card layout and mobile scanning.
item.content:encoded:
This demo post exists to verify a cover-driven page bundle in Pocket Hugo Theme. The image is declared explicitly in front matter so homepage cards, article headers, SEO metadata, and related-content blocks all point to the same asset.

To be, or not to be, that is the question. Whether ’tis nobler in the mind to suffer. The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune.

A mobile-first card layout should still feel calm on a small screen. Short paragraphs, a visible cover, and restrained metadata usually make the reading flow easier to scan.

This paragraph is filler text on purpose. It gives the homepage, archives page, and taxonomy pages enough material to show varied summaries without depending on a real production article.

item.title: A Room for Drafts
item.description: A bilingual bundle demo with a reflective editorial note.
item.content:encoded:
This demo post exists to verify a cover-driven page bundle in Pocket Hugo Theme. The image is declared explicitly in front matter so homepage cards, article headers, SEO metadata, and related-content blocks all point to the same asset.

To be, or not to be, that is the question. Whether ’tis nobler in the mind to suffer. The slings and arrows of outrageous fortune.

A mobile-first card layout should still feel calm on a small screen. Short paragraphs, a visible cover, and restrained metadata usually make the reading flow easier to scan.

This paragraph is filler text on purpose. It gives the homepage, archives page, and taxonomy pages enough material to show varied summaries without depending on a real production article.
